2025.name: Hack Week #1 — Hack me if you can
lessonsLearned: First hack week. Allowed solo projects — later changed to teams-only for Jan 2026.
4-day format (Mon kickoff, Tue-Wed build, Thu demos). Felt compressed.
Winners announced weeks later at Town Hall + Slack. Next time: announce faster.
Feedback survey sent — good practice to continue.

2026.name: Hack Week #2
lessonsLearned: Holiday scheduling was painful. MLK Day + BLR Republic Day created conflicts. Needed async schedules across offices (SF Mon-Fri, BLR Tue-Mon).
Flight/hotel budgets for remote employees were too tight on short notice. Increase buffer or book earlier.
Cross-functional team requirement (no more than 50% from same eng team) drove better collaboration.
8 teams participated, strong output. 'Teams shipped full products within three days. Not prototypes.' (Rishabh's LinkedIn post)
Mid-week demos + dinner was a great energy boost.
Productionization guidance on Day 4 was helpful. Several teams needed to scope down for v1.
Winner announcement delayed because Bharat and Shreyas were both out for scheduled Town Hall. Lock in announcement date and ensure judges available.
Loom recordings essential for async visibility across offices and non-engineering staff.
Late-night dinner policy (originally 8pm, adjusted to 6pm) was well-received.

2026.name: Hack Week #3

events: Set up shared Spotify Jam playlist
Run demo sessions per office (SF and BLR)
Post shipping guidance: (1) Productionize, (2) Support with PR reviews, (3) Record Loom once shipped
Help scope down to MVP if needed
Ensure all Loom links added to spreadsheet

rubric.description: Originality, feasibility, and potential impact of the project concept
Architecture, reliability, test coverage, and production-readiness
Clarity, engagement, and polish of the demo presentation

prizeCategories.category: Best Internal Tool / Most Useful DevTool

teamFormationRules: Cross-functional: more than 50% cannot be from the same engineering team
PMs and Designers encouraged to participate

budget.travelNote: Budget $550-600 for flights, $350/night hotels in SF per Jan 2026 lesson
budget.foodNote: Daily lunches for SF and BLR offices + team dinner + happy hour

schedule.text: Pick the dates — Avoid US and India holidays, check OOO schedules, BLR office holidays
schedule.lesson: Jan 2026: Holiday conflicts with MLK Day and BLR holiday caused last-minute schedule changes.

schedule.text: Decide on cross-office schedule (SF & BLR async alignment)
Confirm the daily structure (Build → Demo → Optimize/Ship)
Cancel all internal meetings for hack week
Set up on-call schedule for customer-facing coverage
Block calendars with hack week events

teams.text: Set team formation rules (2-4 people, cross-functional >50%, no solo, PMs/Designers encouraged)
Set deadline for team/idea submission (~1 week before)
Send reminder nudges for team formation
Have Bharat/Shreyas review and approve all ideas
Lock all teams and ideas (~3 days before)
Decide on prizes and categories (AI, Customer Impact, Internal Tool)

teams.lesson: Jan 2026: Both Bharat and Shreyas were absent for scheduled Town Hall announcement.

logistics.text: Get budget approved — prizes, travel, food, social events (Evelyn)
Assign a BLR logistics lead (Kunal/Sriram + Shiksha)
Arrange travel for remote employees (budget $550-600 flights, $350/night hotel)
logistics.lesson: Jan 2026: $400 flight budget and $1,400 hotel budget were too tight.

logistics.text: Plan social events — team dinner (mid-week) + happy hour (end of week), both offices
Set up late-night dinner policy (expense dinner if working past 6pm)
Distribute prizes ($1,000 per winning team, split among members)

comms.text: Draft and send kick-off announcement in #dev and #general (have Bharat post)
Encourage daily progress updates in Slack
Post hack week recap in #general with all team names, Loom links, winners
Create LinkedIn content — coordinate timing with marketing
Feature hack week at offsite/company events if applicable

judging.text: Judges review all demos and Looms (2-3 days)
Announce winners at Town Hall (lock date + ensure judges available!)
judging.lesson: Jan 2026: Both Bharat and Shreyas were absent for scheduled Town Hall announcement.

judging.text: Have winning teams re-present polished demos to company
Hold planning retro (Sophie + Bharat + BLR leads)
Update master checklist with lessons learned
Track which projects made it to production (2-4 weeks later)

commsTemplates.name: Kick-off Announcement
commsTemplates.template:
:rocket: *Hack Week is happening!*
Hack Week is officially on the calendar :tada:
A full week to build, demo, and ship something bold.

*Dates:* {{dates}}
*Slack:* {{slackChannel}}

*Structure (5 days total):*
:hammer_and_wrench: *Build:* 2 days
:microphone: *Demo:* 1 day
:ship: *Optimize & Ship:* 2 days

*Prize categories:*
:robot_face: *Best Use of AI*
:boom: *Highest Customer Impact*
:toolbox: *Best Internal Tool*

:busts_in_silhouette: *How it works:*
• Teams only (no solo projects)
• PMs + Designers highly encouraged
• Slack-first communication in {{slackChannel}}

Sign up here: {{signupLink}}
More details coming soon — start thinking about what you want to build :eyes:

commsTemplates.name: Team Formation Reminder
commsTemplates.template:
:wave: *Hack Week team formation reminder*

Only *{{daysUntilDeadline}} days left* to form your team and submit your idea!

:clipboard: Sign up: {{signupLink}}
:busts_in_silhouette: Teams of 2-4, cross-functional required
:bulb: Ideas can be anything — dev tools, customer features, experiments

Don't have a team yet? Drop a message in {{slackChannel}} — plenty of people looking for teammates.

commsTemplates.name: Opening Day Message
commsTemplates.template:
:alert: Welcome to {{name}}! A couple of reminders as we get started:

1. If you end up working in the office past 6pm this week, you can order and expense dinner on Fermat
2. Standups & demos on the calendar are all canceled for this week — no need to join them!

Hack away! :frog-wow-scroll:

commsTemplates.name: Shipping Guidance (Day 4)
Recap & Winners

schedulingTips: January works well but watch for MLK Day and BLR Republic Day (Jan 26).
June works well but watch for July 4th proximity.
December is risky — holiday season makes it hard to get clean 5-day window. Push to early January instead.
